api: Microsoft Graph API v1.0 (SharePoint list/listItem resources)
scope: Pass B full-surface expansion. Full documented surface of the SharePoint list/listItem Graph resources scoped to a single site's /sites/{site-id}/lists[/{list-id}/items] hierarchy (Microsoft Graph's list/listItem resource docs, https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/api/resources/list and https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/api/resources/listitem, cross-checked against the microsoftgraph/microsoft-graph-docs-contrib source markdown for exact method/path text). Every documented list/listItem CRUD operation is accounted for below, covered_by a stream/write or excluded with a real reason; capabilities.write flips to true for the 4 new write actions. Column-definition and content-type sub-resources (metadata about a list's schema, not list DATA) and document-set-version/analytics/delta/activity sub-resources are out of scope — this connector reads/writes list rows, not list schema or Graph's activity-analytics surface.

excluded.reason: single-list detail fetch for a resource the lists stream (GET /sites/{site-id}/lists) already lists in full; no additional field is reachable only through this endpoint
single-item detail fetch for a resource the list_items stream (GET /sites/{site-id}/lists/{list-id}/items) already lists in full

Graph documents this as the SAME 'Update listItem' operation as PATCH .../items/{item-id}/fields (listitem-update.md lists both under one method); update_list_item already covers list-item mutation via the fields sub-resource, which is the practically useful shape for writing column data — the bare item-properties PATCH has no additional column data this doesn't already reach

deletes an entire list (moved to the site recycle bin, but every item/column/content-type it holds becomes inaccessible through the live API); legacy exposed zero mutation capability at all, and this expansion's write additions are scoped to the reversible/low-risk create+update surface, not whole-list deletes

a hard delete of a list item (moved to the site recycle bin but immediately gone from the live API's items collection); out of scope for the same reversible/low-risk-only write-expansion reasoning as list deletion
site-level column-definition metadata (a list's SCHEMA, not its data rows) is a distinct sub-resource from the list/listItem data surface this connector targets
list-level column-definition metadata (schema, not data); same reasoning as the site-level columns endpoint
creates a new COLUMN (schema change), not a data row; out of scope for a data-read/write connector
deletes a column definition (schema change affecting every item in the list), not a data row; out of scope for both the schema/data distinction above and the destructive-delete exclusion reasoning
content-type schema metadata, not list item data
version-history sub-resource of a single item; a distinct read surface from the current-value item/fields data this connector's list_items stream already covers

Graph's delta-query change-tracking surface for list items requires a stateful delta-token/skiptoken handoff the engine's declarative cursor-pagination dialect does not model (it is not a page-number/offset/next-url/cursor-token-in-body shape); list_items' own incremental.cursor_field already gives sync-mode-eligible change detection for this connector's purposes
Graph's itemAnalytics usage/activity-statistics sub-resource, not list item data
Graph's itemActivity recent-activity sub-resource, not list item data
